message:
fix: keyboard nav gaps, Playwright auth token pattern, error-resilience assertion (#9723)

Fixes three issues in a single commit:

#9690 — Keyboard navigation gaps (accessibility)
- GPUNamespaceAllocations.tsx: clickable namespace rows lacked role="button",
  tabIndex={0}, onKeyDown, and aria-label; added all four.
- ResourceUsage.tsx: clickable gauge area lacked role="button", tabIndex={0},
  onKeyDown, and aria-label; added all four.
- PodIssues.test.tsx: CardListItem mock div lacked role="button"/tabIndex;
  added both so the test harness matches the production component contract.

#9721 — AIMode.spec.ts uses page.evaluate() to set auth token
Replace page.goto('/login') + page.evaluate(localStorage.setItem) with
page.addInitScript(), consistent with the pattern documented in #9096 and
already applied to Sidebar.spec.ts, Tour.spec.ts, and 10+ other spec files.
page.evaluate() runs after page scripts execute, which is too late for
WebKit/Safari where the auth guard fires synchronously — causing the tests to
run without a valid token and producing false positives.

#9722 — MIN_EXECUTED_CHECKS = 2 allows 3 of 5 checks to silently skip
Raise MIN_EXECUTED_CHECKS from 2 to 4. The suite has 5 resilience tests
(API 500, timeout, partial failure, SSE disconnect, auth expiry). The old
threshold of 2 meant CI remained green even when 60% of checks skipped due
to backend connectivity issues. Requiring 4 of 5 to execute provides a
meaningful signal while still tolerating a single environment flake.

Fixes #9690, Fixes #9721, Fixes #9722

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
